title: Field
statement:
A \emph{field} is a commutative ring with unity $(F, +, \cdot)$
satisfying:

- [label=\textbf{F\arabic*.}]
- \textbf{Additive abelian group.}
 $(F, +)$ is an abelian group with identity $0$.
- \textbf{Multiplicative abelian group on nonzero elements.}
 $(F \setminus \{0\}, \cdot)$ is an abelian group with identity $1$.
 Explicitly: for each $a \in F$ with $a \neq 0$, there exists
 $a^{-1} \in F$ such that $a \cdot a^{-1} = 1$.
- \textbf{Distributivity.}
 $a \cdot (b + c) = a \cdot b + a \cdot c$
 for all $a, b, c \in F$.
- \textbf{Non-triviality.}
 $0 \neq 1$.
